channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: US links Taco Bell lettuce to diarrhea-causing parasite outbreak
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
US officials confirm Taco Bell’s shredded iceberg lettuce from Mexico caused a cyclosporiasis outbreak in five states, sickening over 1,600 people.
Read More: https://punchng.com/us-links-taco-bell-lettuce-to-diarrhea-causing-parasite-outbreak/

item.title: Only God knows next Ogun governor between Adebutu, Yayi — Daniel
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former Ogun State Governor Gbenga Daniel states only God knows the next governor between Adebutu and Yayi for Ogun 2027, urging peace amid political chants
Read More: https://punchng.com/only-god-knows-next-ogun-governor-between-adebutu-yayi-daniel/

item.title: VIDEO: Why Funke Akindele was absent from my wedding – Actor Tobi Makinde
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nollywood actor Tobi Makinde clarifies why Funke Akindele was absent from his wedding, dismissing rift speculations and explaining her film promotion commi
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-why-funke-akindele-was-absent-from-my-wedding-actor-tobi-makinde/

item.title: Police secure conviction of 16 for vandalising govt property in Delta
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Delta State Police Command secures conviction of 16 suspects for vandalising government property, theft, and drug possession. Read more on the Delta vandal
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-secure-conviction-of-16-for-vandalising-govt-property-in-delta/

item.title: CBN Unveils Electronic Portal, Tightens Rules for BDCs’ FX Purchases
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has introduced a new regulatory framework for the purchase of foreign exchange by Bureau De Change (BDC) operators through Authorised Dealer Banks in the Nigerian Foreign Exchange Market (NFEM), unveiling an electronic portal as part of efforts to enhance transparen

item.title: France’s Deschamps set to bow out after 14 years with Zidane in line
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Didier Deschamps ends 14-year reign as France coach after World Cup third-place play-off, with Zinedine Zidane set to take over.
Read More: https://punchng.com/frances-deschamps-set-to-bow-out-after-14-years-with-zidane-in-line/

item.title: Niger investigates suspected infectious disease after child’s death
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Mr Idris said the Ministry of Health responded after a father shared videos on social media alleging that a strange illness had affected members of his household. The post Niger investigates suspected infectious disease after child’s death appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Man Utd latest: Ederson signs new Atalanta deal
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: World Cup final: Tackling Messi ‘huge challenge’ for Spain, says Merino
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "Being able to play against a side like Argentina, which has already won this trophy, makes the match even more significant, and I'm very happy to be experiencing this moment," Merino told reporters at Spain's training base in East Hanover, New Jersey. The post World Cup final: Tackling Messi &#8216

item.title: NUPRC urges new licence holders to support Nigeria’s 2mbpd oil production target
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Obas Esiedesa, Abuja The Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) has urged newly awarded holders of Petroleum Prospecting Licences (PPLs) to fast-track the development of their assets to support the Federal Government’s target of increasing crude oil production to two mill

item.title: Court Jails Man Eight Years Over N90m Money Laundering In Lagos
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal High Court in Ikoyi, Lagos, has sentenced Fidelix Samuel Ebuka to eight years and three months’ imprisonment for laundering N90.9 million. The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) disclosed the conviction in a post on its X page on Friday, saying Justice Yellim Bogoro fo

item.title: FG Seeks Stronger Partnership With Faith-Based Groups As KSM Launch N2bn Endowment Fund
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government has called on faith-based organisations to deepen collaboration with government in advancing national development, social justice and poverty alleviation, declaring that sustainable progress requires collective action across all sectors. Secretary to the Government of the Fede

item.title: ADC aspirant drags party to court over alleged exclusion from primary election
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: An aspirant of the African Democratic Congress, ADC, for the Rivers State House of Assembly, Khana Constituency I seat, Legborsi Nwiabu, has taken his party before a Federal High Court sitting in Port Harcourt, alleging that he was excluded from the party’s last primary election. Also named as

item.title: Spain must rise to Messi challenge in World Cup final — Merino
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Mikel Merino discusses Spain’s “huge challenge” of neutralising Lionel Messi in the World Cup final against Argentina.

item.title: 2027: Wike declares Abuja ‘shut’ to opposition, dares them to fault Tinubu’s projects
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Omeiza Ajayi ABUJA: Minister of the Federal Capital Territory FCT, Nyesom Wike, on Friday declared that President Bola Tinubu’s extensive infrastructure delivery in Abuja has effectively silenced political critics, insisting that visible performance remains the strongest response to opposit

item.title: ‘We Need Her Body For Burial’, Mary Habila’s Family Petitions IGP
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: According to the lawyer, the family has complied with all procedures and conditions stipulated by the Nigeria Police Force for the release of the body but is yet to receive approval to proceed with burial arrangements. The post ‘We Need Her Body For Burial’, Mary Habila’s Family Petition

item.description:
Three persons died and four were injured in a fatal Anambra road crash involving a commercial truck that lost control, somersaulted, and caught fire near A

Omoyele Sowore, AAC presidential candidate, pledges to reverse the naming of public facilities after President Tinubu and other officials if elected in 202

item.title: 2027: NNPP’s Onu warns against abuse of state police
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By John Alechenu, Abuja The Deputy National Chairman of the New Nigeria People’s Party (NNPP), Prince Nweze Onu, has cautioned that the newly passed State Police Bill could be abused by politicians and undermine Nigeria’s democracy ahead of the 2027 general elections. In a statement issu

item.title: AI for good, co-creating the future: China’s vision and actions for global AI governance
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: By Professor Chen Zhimin, Vice President of Fudan University, Shanghai, and Executive Director of the Center for Global AI Innovative Governance (CGAIG).   The 2026 World AI Conference and High-Level Meeting on Global AI Governance will be held in Shanghai from July 17 to 20. /VCG Artificial in

item.title: Bauchi Dissolves Wikki Tourists Management After Club’s Relegation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Bauchi State governor, Bala Mohammed, has dissolved the management committee of Wikki Tourists Football Club with immediate effect following the club’s poor performance in the 2025/2026 Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) season, which ended in its relegation to the Nigeria National League

item.title: Institute Urges Firms To Embed Diversity, Inclusion In Business Strategy
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Chartered Institute of Personnel Management of Nigeria (CIPM) has urged organisations to move beyond treating diversity and inclusion as policy statements and instead integrate them into their core business strategies to drive innovation, productivity and sustainable growth. The Institute made t

item.title: NAICOM warns insurers ahead of July 31 recapitalisation deadline
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Rosemary Iwunze The National Insurance Commission (NAICOM) has warned insurance operators that the July 31 recapitalisation deadline is mandatory, urging companies yet to meet the new minimum capital requirements to act with urgency. The Commissioner for Insurance and Chief Executive Officer of N

item.title: BREAKING: Nasarawa University Lauds DSS For Rescuing Kidnapped Professor
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The management of the Nassarawa State University, Keffi, has applauded the Department of State Services (DSS) for rescuing the school’s dean, Faculty of Agriculture, Professor Samuel Okunsebor. The dean, who was kidnapped by armed men at about midnight on Wednesday at Mile Uku, a boundary comm

item.title: FG Launches New Price Guarantee Scheme For 2m Farmers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: … Flags-off fertiliser distribution to farmers   The federal government on Friday launched a new Guaranteed Minimum Price (GMP) model for two million smallholder farmers while flagging off the distribution of 10 million bags of fertiliser under the Renewed Hope Smallholder Support and Val

item.title: NRC MD commends police for recovering rail assets
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Managing Director of the Nigerian Railway Corporation (NRC), Dr. Kayode Opeifa, has reaffirmed the Corporation’s commitment to strengthening collaboration with the Nigeria Police Force and other security agencies to protect railway assets, describing the nation’s railway infrastructu

item.title: CSOs, Northern Youth Reject Calls For Umahi’s Resignation, Demand Thorough Probe Into Mary’s Death
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Coalition of Civil Societies for Good Governance (CCSGG), in collaboration with the Northern Youth Association of Nigeria (NYAN), has rejected calls for the resignation of the Minister of Works, Engr. David Nweze Umahi, over allegations linked to the death of 26-year-old nurse, Mary Habila. Mary

item.title: Apple and Google ordered to purge ‘nudify’ apps from App Stores
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: In letters sent to Apple and Google, San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu said that both companies have long been aware that they are hosting apps in violation of state law.

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Five Nigerian bank holding companies may need to raise a combined N971.764 billion in equity capital to comply with the read more Five bank holding companies face N972bn capital raise under proposed CBN rules

item.title: Drive Implementation Of Nutrition 774 At Local Levels, Shettima Charges ALGON Excos
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Vice President Kashim Shettima has charged Local government Chairmen to drive the implementation of the Nutrition 774 (N-774) Initiative at the grassroots to improve nutritional outcomes in communities across Nigeria. Accordingly, he directed the Association of Local Governments of Nigeria (ALGON) t

item.title: Family Petitions Makinde, Olubadan Over Erection Of Structures On Ancestors’ Graves
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Adegbenla family of Gbenla compound, in Ibadan North Local Government Area of Oyo State has raised the alarm over erection of structures on the graveyards of their ancestors by some suspected land grabbers. The family in a petition signed by the Mogaji of Adegbenla compound, Chief Kazeem Ishola-

item.title: NITDA Hands Over Public Key Infrastructure to NIMC Under New Identity Law
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Information Technology Development Agency (NITDA) has began the transfer of Nigeria’s Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) to the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC), marking a major step in implementing the newly enacted National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) Act 20

item.title: Strait of Hormuz crossings drop to three-week low as security fears maim shipping
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, the narrow waterway that carries more than 20 percent of global oil and read more Strait of Hormuz crossings drop to three-week low as security fears maim shipping

item.title: Treasuries Post Weekly Advance as Inflation Optimism Prevails
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Treasury market eked out a gain this week as supportive inflation data prevailed over a rebound in oil prices, leading traders to mostly abandon bets on a July Federal Reserve interest-rate hike.

item.title: President Xi Jinping attends Opening Ceremony of the 2026 World AI Conference and High Level Meeting on Global AI Governance
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: …Delivers keynote address From Aidoghie Paulinus, Abuja Chinese President Xi Jinping, on the morning of July 17, 2026, attended the opening ceremony of the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence (AI) Conference and High-Level Meeting on Global AI Governance at the Grand Halls in Shanghai and deliv

item.title: NSIB, Benue Partner To Boost Transport Safety
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Safety Investigation Bureau (NSIB), and the Benue State Government have taken steps to strengthen their partnership on transportation safety, institutional capacity building and technical cooperation as part of efforts to develop the state’s maritime and blue economy sector.

item.title: ‘I am at a loss’: My brother is dying. We are not close. What responsibility do I have to help him?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “He is now no longer able to think clearly or communicate his wishes.”

item.title: State Police Is A Constitutional Necessity, Says SDP’s Adebayo

item.description: Adebayo said fears of abuse should instead be addressed through professionalism, constitutional oversight The post State Police Is A Constitutional Necessity, Says SDP’s Adebayo appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Father Arrested For Allegedly Burning Daughter’s Private Part With Hot Iron Over Bedwetting
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎The Ondo State Police Command has arrested a man, Amos Paul, for allegedly using a hot pressing iron to burn the private part of his 10-year-old daughter as punishment for bedwetting. ‎The suspect, a resident of Unaun, Upenme in Owo Local Government Area of Ondo State, was arrested after the incide

item.title: Kano Consumer Protection Council seizes counterfeit goods worth over N300m
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Kano State Consumer Protection Council (KSCPC) says it has confiscated counterfeit and substandard goods valued at more than N300 million over the past three months as part of its ongoing enforcement operations across the state. The disclosure was contained in a statement issued on Friday&#

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The California-based company is one of the largest producers of salad greens in the U.S.

item.title: Police hunt teacher over alleged flogging death of student in Anambra
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Vincent Ujumadu The Anambra State Police Command has launched a manhunt for a teacher accused of allegedly flogging a 12-year-old student to death at a school in Adazi-Ani, Anaocha Local Government Area of the state. The command said it has also activated its Safe School Initiative, headed by the

item.title: Will the Canadian wildfire smoke affect Spain vs Argentina World Cup final?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: World Cup organisers are monitoring the situation 'closely', with US and FIFA presidents meeting on Friday.

item.description: The Federal Government has stepped up efforts to strengthen Nigeria’s agricultural sector as the Nigerian Agricultural Insurance Corporation and the National Insurance Commission agreed to deepen collaboration to expand agricultural insurance, boost farmers’ confidence and enhance nation

item.title: Court dismisses suit seeking INEC’s recognition for Wabara, Turaki as PDP leaders
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The judge, who affirmed the leadership of Mohammed Abdulrahman-led PDP - the faction loyal to the FCT Minister, Nyesom Wike - said “the preliminary objections filed by all the defendants succeed.” The post Court dismisses suit seeking INEC’s recognition for Wabara, Turaki as PDP leaders appeared fir

item.title: More Canadian wildfire smoke shrouds US midwest, mid-Atlantic and north-east
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: 109 million people face another day of poor air quality as smoke from blazes in Ontario drifts over the USSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Tens of millions of Americans are enduring another day of smoky skies, irritated eyes and bad air quality, as Canadian wildfire smoke spread aga

item.title: Why Trump’s speech on U.S. elections may have been bad for markets
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The president focused in part on accusing China of “sinister election meddling” in 2020.

item.title: Anambra Court jails native doctor, Onyeze Jesus, for false claims of supernatural powers
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Vincent Ujumadu An Anambra State High Court sitting in Awka has sentenced a self-acclaimed prophet and native doctor, Onyebuchi Okocha, popularly known as Onyeze Jesus, to six years’ imprisonment for offences under the Anambra Homeland Security Law. The court also imposed a fine of ₦20 mill

item.title: Bandits kill 13 in fresh attacks on Sokoto communities
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: No fewer than 13 people have been killed in fresh attacks by suspected bandits on three communities in Sabon Birni Local Government Area of Sokoto State, despite efforts by security operatives to repel an attempted invasion of another community. The attacks, which occurred between Thursday evening a

item.title: WAFCON 2026: Super Falcons, Ghana friendly called off
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The planned pre-2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations, WAFCON, international friendly between Nigeria’s Super Falcons and Ghana’s Black Queens has been called off. According to information available on the Super Falcons’ official X account, the game was called off due to unfores

item.title: Super Falcons vs Ghana Friendly Game Called Off
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: The friendly match between Nigeria’s Super Falcons and the Black Queens of Ghana has been called off. This was disclosed on the X handle of the Super Falcons. In a statement on the Super Falcons handle the game, which was billed for Saturday, 8 July in Casablanca, was called off due to unforeseen ci

item.title: Deschamps set for bittersweet ending to France reign as Zidane waits
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Didier Deschamps brings the curtain down on a remarkable 14-year reign as France coach on Saturday when Les Bleus face England in the World Cup third-place play-off. The post Deschamps set for bittersweet ending to France reign as Zidane waits appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: UNICEF chief calls for greater investment in Nigerian children
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Executive Director of the United Nations International Children’s Emergency Fund (UNICEF), Catherine Russell, has urged Nigeria to sustain and expand investments in children, saying the country’s young population holds the key to its future prosperity and development. Russell made th

item.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted by social media consultant Matt Navarra. The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells The Verge. YouTube has been

item.title: Tinubu mourns dame Amaechi, mother of ex-transport minister Rotimi Amaechi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Johnbosco Agbakwuru, Abuja President Bola Tinubu has expressed deep sorrow over the death of Dame Ezinne Mary Oduah Amaechi, mother of former Rivers State Governor and former Minister of Transportation, Rt. Hon. Rotimi Chibuike Amaechi. Dame Amaechi died at the age of 89. In a condolence message,

item.title: Stick with Tuchel unless Guardiola is available - Rooney
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Wayne Rooney thinks the Football Association should stick with Thomas Tuchel as England head coach unless a move for Pep Guardiola is possible.

item.description: Governor Abba Yusuf of Kano State on Friday received a Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Senator Rufai Hanga, in his Abuja office. The visit came as as defection talks to the All Progressives Congress, APC, advance Senator Hanga reportedly considering leaving the NDC after losing out on the Kano Cen

item.title: Hamilton hopeful of closing 'bigger than anticipated' Ferrari Spa deficit
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Lewis Hamilton says he is hopeful Ferrari can close on their rivals as the Belgian Grand Prix weekend progresses despite admitting the Italian team's deficit in Friday practice was "bigger than anticipated".

item.title: Police rescue kidnap victim, kill suspect in Kogi forest
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Kogi State Police Command has rescued a kidnapped victim, neutralised a suspected kidnapper, and recovered an Italian-made pump-action gun during a joint security operation in Ayegunle Gbede, Ijumu Local Government Area of the state. The victim, identified as Abubakar Kabiru, was reportedly abdu

item.title: Japa: NMA Chairmanship Aspirant Urges FG To Implement CBA To Curb Menace

item.title: Mary Habila: Police spokesperson urges restraint, warns against spread of unverified allegations
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The spokesperson for the Nigeria Police Force Headquarters Annex, Lagos, CSP Nkeiruka Nwode, has appealed to Nigerians to exercise restraint and refrain from spreading unverified allegations over the death of Miss Mary Habila. In a statement on Friday, Nwode described Habila’s death as a heart

item.description: The Ondo State Police Command has arrested a father in Owo Local Government Area for allegedly causing grievous bodily harm on his 10-year old daughter. The command’s spokesperson, SP Abayomi Jimoh, disclosed in a statement issued on Thursday, July 16, 2026, that the suspect, Amos Paul, used a hot p

item.title: South African Police Arrest 69 At Violent Anti-Immigration Protest
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Weeks of protests to demand that undocumented foreign nationals leave South Africa have already claimed the lives of at least four foreigners, according to police. The post South African Police Arrest 69 At Violent Anti-Immigration Protest appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Site workers, residents escape death as three buildings collapse in Abia
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A one-storey building under construction has collapsed on Finbars Road, Umuahia, causing panic in the area. The building collapsed in the early hours of Friday following a torrential downpour, though no lives were lost. The incident occurred about two months after officials of the Umuahia Capital De

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …workers yet to get 10% equity 13 years after sale …accuses ex-manager of sponsored campaign against Ajaero …insists N409.9bn from sale went to workers’ severance, not union leaders The Nigeria Labour Congress (NLC) has again reignited the controversy surrounding the privatis

item.title: We’ll appeal Abuja FHC judgment — Turaki-led PDP
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Interim National Working Committee (INWC) of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) has said it will appeal Friday’s Abuja Federal High Court (FHC) judgment, which declined to recognise the Tanimu Turaki-led executive as the authentic leadership of the party. The Board of Trustees (BoT) of the

item.title: Ododo Receives Rescued Kidnap Victims, Vows No Ransom For Criminals
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Kogi State Governor, Alhaji Ahmed Usman Ododo, on Friday, received four rescued kidnap victims at the Government House, Lokoja, vowing that all perpetrators of the crime would be apprehended and prosecuted. The victims, comprising a school principal, a NECO official, and two female candidates, were

item.title: ‘I’m saddened’ – Peter Obi mourns death of Amaechi’s mother
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, presidential candidate, Peter Obi, has mourned the death of the mother of the African Democratic Congress, ADC, vice presidential candidate, Rotimi Amaechi. In a post on his verified X handle on Friday, Obi said he was saddened and touched by the news of he

item.title: Some England players bemused by Tuchel subs in Argentina defeat
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: A number of England players - including more than one senior player - were bemused by Thomas Tuchel's defensive substitutions in the World Cup semi-final against Argentina, Sky Sports News understands.

item.title: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says his 30-day warranty is all about trust
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says buyers of its new e-paper smartwatches should know what they're signing up for and trust Pebble to make things right if they run into issues, despite the short warranty. "I think the most important thing is trust," Migicovsky told me in an interview this week. "Do

item.title: Lasaco Assurance turns tide on half-year loss, helped by cost efficiency
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Its return to profitability owed not so much to revenue growth as to cost cutting, as insurance revenue, its core income source, retreated by 3.2 per cent from the half-year 2025 level to N16.3 billion. The post Lasaco Assurance turns tide on half-year loss, helped by cost efficiency appeared first

item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: In the name of Allah, Most Merciful, the Bestower of Mercy. All praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the worlds and Lord of all creation — may the salutations of Allah, His peace and blessings be upon the noblest of all of the Prophets and Messengers, Muhammad, upon his family, his Companions and [&#

item.title: Ederson extends Atalanta contract after Man Utd move collapses
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Atalanta midfielder Ederson extends his contract after a move to Manchester United collapses.

item.title: Osun 2026: Oyebamiji Unveils Mechanised Farming Agenda
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Promises Rural Road Network The governorship candidate of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in the August 15, 2026 Osun State governorship election, Asiwaju Munirudeen Bola Oyebamiji (AMBO), has pledged to prioritise mechanised agriculture and rural infrastructure as part of efforts to tran

item.title: 'Police protection and threats' | Eckert reveals impact of Spygate scandal
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Speaking exclusively to Sky Sports' Mark McAdam, Southampton boss Tonda Eckert reveals the challenges he has faced after the Spygate scandal that resulted in their expulsion from the Championship play-offs.

item.title: Heavy rain, windstorm leave three dead, hundreds displaced in Yobe
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Following a heavy downpour accompanied by a windstorm that swept through several communities, three people have reportedly been confirmed dead, while houses and property worth millions of naira were destroyed in Nguru Local Government Area of Yobe State. The disaster, which occurred overnight, affec

item.title: 2027: Appraising Bauchi’s five-horse guber race, By Kola Oyerinde
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Many observers conclude that, on the question of who should succeed Bala Mohammed and safeguard the legacy of good governance that he established, the joke is substantially on M A Abubakar and Ibrahim Kashim. The post 2027: Appraising Bauchi’s five-horse guber race, By Kola Oyerinde appeared first o

item.title: Osun guber: APC’s Oyebamiji vows inclusive governance, others
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) governorship candidate for the August 15 election in Osun State, Bola Oyebamiji, has promised to run an inclusive administration that will ensure equitable development across all parts of the state if elected. Speaking during campaign rallies at Ibokun and Ijebu-J

item.title: Taraba Head of Civil Service urges casual workers to apply for recruitment exercise
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Head of the Taraba State Civil Service, Dr Hamidu Ahmed Kara, has urged all casual workers across the state to apply for the ongoing recruitment exercise being conducted by the Taraba State Civil Service Commission (CSC). The appeal comes after Governor Agbu Kefas approved the recruitment of 1,8

item.title: Court rejects bid to force INEC to recognise Turaki-led PDP leadership ahead of 2027 elections
item.dc:creator: Ripples Nigeria
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has dismissed a suit seeking to compel the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to recognise the Tanimu Turaki (SAN)-led interim leadership of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), dealing a setback to the faction led by the party’s Board of Trustees C

item.title: ‘I’ll miss the French team’ – Deschamps ahead of England clash
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: Didier Deschamps said Friday that no tears will be shed as he prepares to take charge of his final France match in the World Cup third-place play-off against England. The post ‘I’ll miss the French team’ – Deschamps ahead of England clash appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Akwa Ibom: Police exhume corpse of slain victim of mob attack, arrest suspects
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Akwa Ibom State Police Command says its operatives have exhumed the corpse of one Emmanuel Effiong, 21, a victim of a mob attack in Mbak 4 Community, Uyo Local Government Area of the state. The command added that four suspects, David Okon, alias Ita, 45; Joseph Gibson, 39; Essien Solomon, 23; and Im

item.title: 9 Victims Escape As Abductors Doze Off in Katsina Forest
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Troops of the Joint Task Force North West, Operation Fansan Yamma, have rescued nine victims who escaped from their abductors, recovered 56 rustled livestock and arrested a suspected terrorist during separate intelligence-led operations in Katsina State. The Media Information Officer of the Joint Ta

item.title: Tinubu Awards 317km FCT Rural Roads, Completes 221, 96 Ongoing – Wike
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Minister, Nyesom Wike, on Friday, said President Bola Tinubu, since assuming office on May 29, 2023, has awarded a total of 317 kilometres of road in Abuja’s satellite towns alone, completed 221km, while 96 kilometers are ongoing at different locations. Wike

item.title: ICE Flight Monitor Interactive Dashboard
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.humanrightsfirst.org/ice-flight-monitor-interactive-dashboard Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48951014 Points: 11 # Comments: 1

item.title: At least three killed and hundreds displaced by heavy rain in Chile
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Chilean President Jose Antonio Kast says that he is travelling to the Biobio region to survey flood damage.

item.title: Edo council boss donates 12 motorcycles to Police
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Executive Chairman of Egor Local Government Council, Hon. Osaro Eribo, has donated 12 motorcycles to The post Edo council boss donates 12 motorcycles to Police appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Kelechi Iheanacho joins Turkey side Bursaspor
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: Nigeria international Kelechi Iheanacho has completed a move to Turkish club Bursaspor after rejecting a new contract offer from Scottish champions Celtic. The post Kelechi Iheanacho joins Turkey side Bursaspor appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: NEMA receives 1,516 Nigerians voluntarily repatriated from South Africa
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: NEMA said the returnees arrived in seven batches through the Murtala Muhammed International Airport (MMIA), Lagos, aboard Air Peace and South African Airways flights. The post NEMA receives 1,516 Nigerians voluntarily repatriated from South Africa appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: FG arraigns three suspects linked to Oriire school abduction
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Federal Government has arraigned three men before the Federal High Court in Abuja over their alleged involvement in the read more FG arraigns three suspects linked to Oriire school abduction

item.title: Iheanacho completes move to Turkish side Bursaspor
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigerian striker Kelechi Iheanacho has completed a move to Turkish second-tier club Bursaspor, ending his brief spell with Scottish champions Celtic ahead of the new season. The post Iheanacho completes move to Turkish side Bursaspor appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: A Closer Look at DualKeyz's "Sacrifice of Praise"
item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: No words on this track at all, but honestly you don't need them. Dualkeyz makes a bold statement in the contemporary gospel scene with his latest instrumental release, "Sacrifice of Praise." By seamlessly blending steady melodic Afrobeat rhythms with a sophisticated gospel aesthetic, Dualkeyz proves
